serving.natural_language_portion: One medium cup of Ribena Roselle Black Tea, approximately 500 ml as typically served at KOI Thé bubble tea outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Sweetened black tea, ready-to-drink — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 14355) and blackcurrant syrup component data; roselle hibiscus tea analogue from peer-reviewed composition data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; supplemented by published roselle beverage composition literature
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for Ribena Roselle Black Tea as served at KOI Thé. Values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA FoodData Central data for sweetened ready-to-drink black tea (FDC ID 171917, ~38 kcal/100 ml), (2) Ribena blackcurrant syrup contribution (approximately 10–15 g sugar per 500 ml serving based on published Ribena product data), and (3) roselle hibiscus tea composition from Mahadevan et al. (2009) and related peer-reviewed sources. The resulting per-100 ml estimate is approximately 35–40 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 9–10 g carbohydrate (mostly sugar). A conservative midpoint of 37 kcal/100 ml is used. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugar-sweetened beverages and black tea — analogous estimate based on sucrose GI and sweetened tea data from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ribena Roselle Black Tea. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) sucrose GI of 65 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) sweetened black tea GI values reported in the range of 45–60 in peer-reviewed literature (Wolever et al., various); (3) dilution effect of tea base and roselle extract. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving calculated as: (9.2 g carbohydrate per 100 ml × 5 × GI 55) / 100 = 25.3 g GL. This is a medium-confidence analogous estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; roselle (hibiscus) has published antihypertensive properties
hypertension.guidance: Ribena Roselle Black Tea contains approximately 5 mg sodium per 100 ml and includes roselle (Hibiscus sabdariffa) extract, which has been associated with modest blood pressure reduction in clinical trials; however, the high sugar content means total daily intake should be moderated for individuals with hypertension who are also managing weight.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (~9 g per 100 ml; ~45 g per 500 ml serving) and low glycemic index (~55) (borderline to medium) producing a high glycemic load per serving (~25)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat Ribena Roselle Black Tea as a high-glycemic-load beverage; a single 500 ml serving contributes approximately 45 g of free sugars and a glycemic load of ~25, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation and is inconsistent with standard dietary management targets for glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk; roselle extract may offer modest cardioprotective antioxidant benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of Ribena Roselle Black Tea is a cardiovascular concern primarily due to its high free sugar content (~45 g per 500 ml serving), which is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk; the roselle component provides anthocyanins with antioxidant properties, but this does not offset the risk from habitual high-sugar beverage intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ribena Roselle Black Tea (利宾纳洛神花红茶) is a sweetened cold tea beverage sold at KOI Thé, a Taiwanese-origin bubble tea chain with outlets across Singapore. The drink combines brewed black tea with roselle (Hibiscus sabdariffa) extract or syrup — which imparts a deep ruby-red colour and a tart, cranberry-like flavour — and Ribena blackcurrant syrup, a well-known British blackcurrant cordial brand, which adds sweetness and a distinctive berry note. The result is a fruity, tangy, and sweet iced tea with a vibrant colour and refreshing character. It is served cold, typically over ice, in a medium or large cup (approximately 500 ml). Nutritionally, the beverage is essentially fat-free and protein-free, with its caloric content derived almost entirely from added sugars (approximately 37 kcal per 100 ml, or ~185 kcal per 500 ml serving). The roselle component contributes anthocyanins and a small amount of vitamin C and potassium. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low, borderline medium), but the glycemic load per 500 ml serving is high (~25) due to the large volume of free sugars. Clinically, this beverage is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing weight, and should be consumed only occasionally by individuals with cardiovascular disease. The roselle (hibiscus) component has been studied for antihypertensive effects, but the sugar content limits its benefit in that context. It is halal, vegan, dairy-free, and gluten-free.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any approved primary source for Ribena Roselle Black Tea as served at KOI Thé. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA sweetened ready-to-drink black tea data combined with published Ribena product sugar content and roselle beverage composition from peer-reviewed literature. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database sucrose and sweetened tea data. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. protein_g is 0.0: naturally zero — this is a sugar-sweetened tea beverage with no protein-containing ingredients. 2. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero — no fat-containing ingredients are present in this beverage formulation. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0: naturally zero by product formulation (fat-free beverage). 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero — the beverage is a liquid extract with no intact fibre. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero — plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1: not reported in the source database for this beverage category. 7. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1: not reported in the source database. 8. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1: not reported in the source database. 9. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1: not reported in the source database. 10. vitamin_b6_mg is -1: not reported in the source database. 11. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1: not reported in the source database. 12. folate_mcg_dfe is -1: not reported in the source database. 13. vitamin_d_mcg is -1: not reported in the source database. 14. vitamin_e_mg is -1: not reported in the source database. 15. phosphorus_mg is -1: not reported in the source database. 16. magnesium_mg is -1: not reported in the source database. 17. zinc_mg is -1: not reported in the source database. 18. selenium_mcg is -1: not reported in the source database. 19. Cuisine is Taiwanese (KOI Thé is a Taiwanese bubble tea brand); multilingual naming rules for Taiwanese cuisine do not mandate Malay, Tamil, or Hindi local names — those are required only for Singaporean dishes. Chinese names are provided as the dish originates from a Chinese-language Taiwanese brand context. 20. GI set to 55 (low) as analogous estimate; glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (9.2 × 5 × 55) / 100 = 25.3. 21. Price SGD 5.10 preserved from input (field survey data, KOI Thé Funan). 22. The food_id GD001324 is preserved from the input seed record.
